title: Accessible Multimodal Routing at Gongbei
summary: A cohort-aware routing model studies how slopes, stairs, transfers, crowding and missing elevators change access through a multimodal border hub.

publish_assets.alt: Multilayer Gongbei transport network connecting pedestrian, bus, rail, and taxi nodes
publish_assets.caption: Multilayer network used in the accessible-routing study.

data_type: Case-study network and configured cohort assumptions
research_question: How do mobility constraints change optimal routes and intervention priorities in a dense multimodal hub?
limitations: The case study covers one 1 km² hub.
Several behavioral and accessibility costs require local calibration.
Policy implications are model-based rather than evidence from a deployed intervention.

title: Interpretable EV Dispatch and Replenishment
summary: A white-box pipeline links physics-informed energy prediction, fairness-aware scheduling and disruption-triggered replanning for an electric fleet.

publish_assets.alt: Profit, energy, and fairness trade-off frontier for the EV dispatch experiment
publish_assets.caption: Non-degenerate scheduling frontier from the reported EV dispatch run.

data_type: Project-local operational records; redistribution status not asserted
research_question: Can an auditable model balance fleet energy, profit, fairness and disruption response?
limitations: The reported energy MAPE is 42.512%, despite a high R².
Scheduling uses a simplified assignment structure rather than an exact full-scale MILP.
Disruptions are simulated with a stylized generator.

title: Climate-Driven Dengue on a Spatial Grid
summary: A reaction–diffusion model combines temperature-dependent mosquito ecology, long-range dispersal, vaccination allocation and numerical checks.

publish_assets.alt: Matrix of simulated dengue incidence across synthetic climate and allocation scenarios
publish_assets.caption: Synthetic incidence comparison generated by the experiment matrix.

research_question: How do climate forcing and spatial allocation assumptions change simulated dengue incidence?
limitations: The published experiment matrix is synthetic.
Outputs are scenario comparisons, not forecasts for a specific city.
Biological parameters require empirical calibration before applied use.

title: Phantom Traffic-Jam Stability
summary: Microscopic traffic experiments use IDM-style car following to examine when small disturbances grow and how mixed vehicle behavior changes the response.

publish_assets.alt: Space-time heat map of speed in a synthetic microscopic traffic simulation
publish_assets.caption: Synthetic traffic-wave heat map.

research_question: Under which modeled conditions do local speed disturbances become persistent stop-and-go waves?
limitations: Driver and controller parameters do not establish performance on a real road network.

title: Coastal Defence Under Rising Tides
summary: A teaching case combines sea-level scenarios, extreme-value storm surges and cost optimization over a 50-year planning horizon.

publish_assets.alt: Modeled coastal defence cost and risk optimization frontier
publish_assets.caption: Optimization frontier for the stylized coastal-defence teaching case.

data_type: Stylized scenarios and simulated storm surges
research_question: How does uncertainty in sea level and storm surge change the least-cost timing of coastal defences?
limitations: The city and damage functions are stylized.
Results are for modeling instruction, not infrastructure design.

title: Pollination Ecosystem Trade-offs
summary: An agent-based C++ simulation compares pollinator populations, agricultural yield and management assumptions across configured scenarios.

publish_assets.alt: Comparison of pollinator population and yield across configured management scenarios
publish_assets.caption: Scenario comparison from the synthetic agent-based ecosystem model.

research_question: How do configured management regimes change simulated yield and pollinator persistence?
limitations: Scenario parameters are not a validated agricultural field study.
Comparisons should not be generalized to policy without calibration and external validation.
reproduce_command: cmake -S pollination_ecosystem_model -B pollination_ecosystem_model/build && cmake --build pollination_ecosystem_model/build --config Release
